subject: Human Resources
title: Rituals and Traditions
description: A micro-lesson that makes Rituals and Traditions usable.

hook.text: Every Friday at 4 PM, the team shares one win from the week. It takes 10 minutes. It transforms morale.

buildup.text: Rituals are recurring practices that create shared identity—team lunches, onboarding ceremonies, or monthly shout-outs.

discovery.text: Research shows rituals strengthen group bonds and belonging—even simple ones like a weekly round of appreciations.

twist.text: Forced fun backfires. The best rituals emerge organically and feel authentic—not mandated by HR as 'culture initiatives.'

climax.text: Rituals that celebrate effort, mark milestones, and connect people become the heartbeat of team culture.

punchline.text: Culture isn't built in off-sites. It's built in small moments, repeated.

quiz.question: What makes workplace rituals effective?
options: They feel authentic and emerge organically
They're mandatory and enforced by HR
